principle.name: Function over form
principle.description: Usability before aesthetics. Every design decision must serve user goals. Beautiful but unusable designs are failures.
principle.rationale: Design exists to enable users to accomplish tasks. Aesthetics support usability; they do not replace it.

principle.name: Every screen needs error, empty, and loading states
principle.description: Every screen in the mockup set must include designs for: error state (when something fails), empty state (when no data), loading state (when fetching).
principle.rationale: Real applications encounter these states constantly. Missing them leads to poor implementation and bad UX.

principle.name: Accessibility is mandatory, not optional
principle.description: WCAG 2.1 AA minimum. Color contrast ratios, font sizes, touch targets, keyboard navigation, and screen reader considerations must be addressed in every design.
principle.rationale: Accessibility is a legal and ethical requirement. Designs that ignore it create barriers for users.

principle.name: Consistent component patterns
principle.description: Use the same button styles, form patterns, card layouts, and navigation patterns across all screens. Document patterns in design-spec.
principle.rationale: Consistency reduces cognitive load and speeds implementation. Inconsistent patterns confuse users and developers.

principle.name: Responsive design
principle.description: Define breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) and ensure mockups demonstrate key screens at each breakpoint.
principle.rationale: Users access applications on many devices. Non-responsive designs fail on mobile.

principle.name: Iterate with user on visual direction
principle.description: Present color palette and typography early. Get user approval before creating full mockups. Avoid large rework by aligning on direction first.
principle.rationale: Visual preferences are subjective. Early alignment prevents wasted effort on rejected aesthetics.

pitfall.why_problematic: Produces designs that look good but are hard to use. Users abandon or struggle with the product.
pitfall.correct_approach: Validate every design choice against user goals. If a choice doesn't improve usability, reconsider it.

pitfall.description: Omitting error, empty, or loading states
pitfall.why_problematic: Implementation teams guess or skip these states. Users see broken or confusing UIs when things fail or load.
pitfall.correct_approach: Include error, empty, and loading states for every screen in the mockup set.

pitfall.why_problematic: Retrofitting accessibility is expensive. Designs that fail contrast or touch targets create exclusion.
pitfall.correct_approach: Check contrast ratios, font sizes, and touch targets during design. Document accessibility decisions.

pitfall.why_problematic: Users and developers are confused. Implementation becomes fragmented.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define component patterns in design-spec and apply them consistently across all mockups.

pitfall.description: Creating mockups before user approves visual direction
pitfall.why_problematic: User may reject palette or typography, requiring full rework of all mockups.
pitfall.correct_approach: Present color-palette and typography first. Get approval. Then create mockups.

category.name: before_mockup_creation
category.item: User stories and UX constraints extracted from PRD.
Color palette and typography approved by user.

category.name: per_screen
category.item: Accessibility requirements met (contrast, font size, touch targets).

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Dark/light mode addressed if PRD requires it.
